### Conversions
For the Rogue Echo Bike:
1000 metres = 0.62 miles
2000 metres = 1.24 miles
4000 metres = 2.48 miles

Row - Airbike - Run Conversion
100 meters Row =  0.13 miles (200 metres) on the Rogue Echo Bike.
It takes an average athlete 24 seconds to row 100M. (4 mins per 1000m)
Rowing 100M burns about 6 calories.
Biking 0.13 miles (200 metres) burns about 4.2 calories.

10 calories Row = 7 calories on the Rogue Echo Bike.
It takes an average athlete 36 seconds to row 10 calories.
10 Rowing Calories is equivalent to 166 meters rowed.
7 Calories on the Echo Bike is about 0.22 miles (354 metres)

In two minutes most people can row 500 meters.
In three minutes most people can Airbike 1 Mile (1600 metres)

10 Calories Rower = 7 Calories Echo Bike
500M Row takes 2 Minutes
1000 Meters Row = 1.3 Miles (2092 metres) on Echo Bike
1 Mile (1600 metres) AirBike takes 3 Minutes

1 mile run (1600 metres) = 2000 metres Row = 2.6 miles (4184 metres)

coach_notes:
- Focus today is on strict precise movement, no kipping, jerking or swinging
- If any exercise is too easy, we add weight, if too hard, we scale it to make it easier
- Class is planned for 1 hour plus stretching which athletes can do alone


### Push-ups Strict (6 mins)

**PoP:**
- Straight line from shoulders to heels, no raising or sagging in the hips
- Head/neck in neutral position, do not lower or raise the head
- Feet together, touching
- Hands underneath and slightly outside the shoulders
- Elbows go backwards diagonally, not out to the sides
- On Timer & Coach's cue

### Strict Push-Ups
Each rep should last 10 seconds, 5 seconds down, 5 seconds up
Min 1: 6 x Push-Ups in 60 secs
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 6 x Weighted Push-Ups in 60 secs
Scale up or down as needed but try and work for the full 60 seconds

Push-Ups scale up: add weight plate on upper back
Choose weight to make it as difficult as possible but make sure you can complete the 6 reps within 60 secs

Push-Ups Scale down:
Sc1: Strict negative, knees down, push-up, raise knees to start position
Sc2: Begin on knees and perform as above
If 6 reps cannot be completed with good form on Sc2, reduce the reps to 5 or 4 accordingly


### Pull-Ups Strict chin over bar (6 mins)
Same format as above where we add weight to make it more difficult or scale it to make it easier
Min 1: 6 x Strict Pull-Ups in 60 seconds (IGYG with partner)
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 6 x Strict Weighted Pull-Ups in 60 seconds (IGYG with partner)
Pull-Ups scale up: add weight plate to weight belt, 
Choose weight to make it as difficult as possible but make sure you can complete the 6 reps within 60 secs

Pull-Ups Scale down:
Banded Pull-Ups - every rep starts from a static position, no bouncing, swinging or kipping


### Parallettes L-Sit Hold (6 mins)
2 rounds of:
Min 1: 10-20 secs unbroken Parallettes L-Sit Hold in 60 secs
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 10-20 secs unbroken Parallettes L-Sit Hold in 60 secs
Scale down as needed but aim for a shorter time (min 10 seconds) with a more difficult scale, rather than a longer time with an easier scale

L-Sits Scale (as in homework):
Rx: Straight legs, feet and legs together, feet @ 90° to hips or higher, shoulders behind wrists
Sc1: Bent knees or straight legs @ < 90°
Sc2: Bent knees in tuck position, shoulders in front of wrists


### KB OH Carry 2 x KB held overhead (6 mins)
2 rounds of:
Min 1: 60 secs unbroken KB OH Carry for metres & weight (light weight)
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 60 secs unbroken KB OH Carry for metres & weight (heavier weight to decide WOD weight)
KB OH Carry
Rx: 2 x ♀ 16kg, ♂ 24kg
Sc1: 2 x ♀ 12kg, ♂ 20kg
Sc2: 2 x ♀ 8kg, ♂ 16kg


## WOD: 2 x EMOM 20 mins total
(60 secs Work + 15 secs change over, add scores together for each movement for an overall total)

### 2 rounds of:

- Min 1: Max Strict Push-Ups
- Min 2: Max Strict Pull-Ups
- Min 3: Max secs Parallettes L-Sit Hold
- Min 4: Max SkiErg Metres
- Min 5: Max metres KB OH Carry
- Min 6: Max Rower Metres
- Min 7 & 8: REST

### NOTES:
These reps/seconds/metres do NOT have to be unbroken
Keep movements consistent with what we practiced during the class
Split large groups up.
Rx/advanced do as written, Scaled do Push-Ups, Pull-Ups, KBOHC, Rower, L-Sit, SkiErg.

sections.content:

* Team: Bumper Plate Burpee Relay
Team WOD Part 1: Bumper Plate Burpee Relay (12 mins)
Set up:
2 Teams line up together (pick randomly which teams go together) facing each other in a line from the 10 metre line to the opposite 10 metre line.
A stack of 16 weight plates are next to the 1st athlete (Each team has 10x 5kg & 6 x 10kg)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
The 1st athlete takes the 1st plate, places it on the ground in front of him and does a burpee with his hands on the plate. He then stands and passes the plate to the next athlete who does the same. This continues until all plates are restacked at the opposite end of the 10 metre line.
Plates can NOT be dropped, they must be placed down

* WB Bear Crawl
Set-up part A:
2 teams of 8 are randomly selected from the teams of 4
Each team begins behind the 10 metre line on opposite sides
32 assorted wallballs are placed in a line in the middle of the box on the 5 metre line
WBs per team: 3x 9kg, 2x 7kg, 3x 6kg, 2x 5kg, 1x 4kg, 1x 3kg (large) 2 x 3kg (small), 2 x 1kg (small)
Task:
On 3-2-1, Go!
1 player at a time must Bear Crawl forwards, and grab a WB WITHOUT USING THEIR HANDS!
The WB must remain underneath the body of the athlete at all times (they cannot kick or knee it forwards and chase after it)
They must turn around and Bear Crawl the WB back to their team
When the WB and athlete crosses the line, the next athlete can go
All Athletes must go at least once

Set-up Part B
Teams set up as in Part A
Prepare 2x 9kg, 2x 7kg, 2x 6kg, 1x 5kg & 1x 4kg Wallballs behind their 10 metre line
From the start to the end of their turn, teams of 2 must be in contact with each other at all times
The partnered athletes can NOT touch the Wallball with their hands at any time during the task
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams of 2 have to transport their Wallballs to the opposite side of the 10 metre line
When their WB is over the line they can return to their team remembering to remain in contact with each other until they cross the start line again

Part C (i)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Each team gets an image (Wallball Clown/Snowman xmas party 2025) which they have to recreate on the ground with the wallballs
Only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor
First to recreate the image wins

Part C (ii)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams have to recreate the image again, but this time, only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor
First to recreate the image wins

Part C (iii)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams have to recreate the image again, but this time, only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor and the clown/snowman MUST be upright
Players can hold/support the WBs in any way they see fit
First to recreate the exact image wins

Team WOD Part 3: * SkiErg + Synchro * Squat Jump
Set-up teams of 4:

Set 4 SkiErgs up in each of the 4 corners of the box facing inwards
Task:
On 3-2-1-Go!
A 1 min timer begins (30 secs rest/change over) and the athlete on the SkiErg begins. At the same time, the rest of the team perform Synchro Squat Jumps
The team must count their reps aloud and all team members must jump .
At the end of the 1 min work there is a 1 min rest
Write down Squat Jumps & Calories after every round
If any of the athletes cannot keep up, they are allowed to take up a Plank Position whilst the rest of the team completes the Squat Jumps.
Teams can only jump all together or if 1 athlete is in a Plank Position.
At least 2 people MUST perform the Squat Jumps
I planned to do 2 rounds but everyone was already done after 1!
Teams will be scored on Calories and Squat Jumps completed.

Team WOD Part 4:
Synchro:
* Push-up Strict
&
* DB/KB Floor to Overhead

Set-up:
All athletes choose a DB/KB that they can perform at least 10 reps unbroken

On 3-2-1-Go! a 5 min Timer begins
All athletes except 1 pick up their DB/KB and Snatch it overhead in synchronisation and then swap hands and repeat
Meanwhile, 1 athlete performs a Push-Up at the same time as the others do the F2OH
They repeat this as many times as they can until 1 or more athletes cannot continue
They can change the Push-Up athlete as many times as they like but Push-Ups & F2OH must always be performed together
Athletes who can do RX Push-Ups are not allowed to scale
Reps must be counted aloud

Prepare 2x 9kg, 2x 7kg, 2x 6kg, 1x 5kg & 1x 4kg Wallballs behind their 10 metre line
From the start to the end of their turn, teams of 2 must be in contact with each other at all times
The partnered athletes can NOT touch the Wallball with their hands at any time during the task
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams of 2 have to transport their Wallballs to the opposite side of the 10 metre line
When their WB is over the line they can return to their team but on their return journey, only 2 feet may touch the floor at any time

Part C (1)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Each team gets an image (Wallball Clown/Snowman xmas party 2025) which they have to recreate on the ground with the wallballs 
Only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor
First to recreate the image wins

Part C (2)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams have to recreate the image again, but this time, only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor
First to recreate the image wins

Team WOD Part 3: SkiErg + Synchro Squat Jumps (20 mins)
Set-up teams of 4:
Set 4 SkiErgs up in each of the 4 corners of the box facing inwards
Task:
On 3-2-1-Go!
A 1 min timer begins and the athlete on the SkiErg begins. At the same time, the rest of the team perform Synchro Squat Jumps
The team must count their reps aloud and all team members must jump .
At the end of the 1 min work there is a 1 min rest
Write down Squat Jumps & Calories after every round
If any of the athletes cannot keep up, they are allowed to take up a Plank Position whilst the rest of the team completes the Squat Jumps.
Teams can only jump all together or if 1 athlete is in a Plank Position.
At least 2 people MUST perform the Squat Jumps
We will perform 2 rounds of this Workout but scores will be calculated after the first and second round separately
Teams will be scored on Calories and Squat Jumps completed.

Team WOD Part 5 (if needed) : Synchro Push-Ups & Alternating DB/KB Floor to Overhead (12 mins)
Set-up:
All athletes choose a DB/KB that they can perform at least 10 reps unbroken

On 3-2-1-Go!  a 5 min Timer begins
All athletes except 1 pick up their DB/KB and Snatch it overhead in synchronisation and then swap hands and repeat
Meanwhile, 1 athlete performs a Push-Up at the same time as the others do the F2OH
They repeat this as many times as they can until 1 or more athletes cannot continue
They can change the Push-Up athlete as many times as they like but Push-Ups & F2OH must always be performed together
Athletes who can do RX Push-Ups are not allowed to scale
Reps must be counted aloud

coach_notes:
# <u>Gymnastic Ring Drills</u>

On a 6 minute clock alternating with partner of same height:
6 x 5-20 secs hold every 30 secs, 30 secs REST
   
**1.** **<u>Rings Top Support</u>**
   
- Set Rings at a height which allows you to touch the ground on tiptoes (usually top part of ring at hip height)
   
- do NOT bend the elbows, push the shoulders away from the ears

**2.** **<u>3x 5 Ring Rows</u>**

- Start with your arms straight and in line with the rings, with your knees bent, and your back parallel to the ground.
- Shoulders should be set under the bar (Rx), but this will be too difficult for most people at first.
- Find a position which makes the movement challenging but manageable
- Perform the Pulling Prep (Scap Pull-Up) before pulling yourself up to the rings
- Pull yourself up as far as you can while keeping your back horizontal, then pull your chest to the rings with palms facing each other
- Make sure that the rings/thumbs contact the outside of the chest on every rep
- Keep elbows as close to the body as possible
- Slowly lower down to the start position

**3.** **<u>2x 5 Unilateral Rows per side</u>**

**4.** **<u>3x 4-6 Reverse Flyes</u>**

- Top of the rings set at hip height

- 3 steps back

- Begin movement with scapular retraction
- Pull yourself up by opening the arms out to the side, keep the arms straight
- Make sure to reset the scapular retraction at the bottom of the movement before each rep
- Keep neck neutral (look at ceiling)

Level 0: 3 steps back, row upwards and lower down (negative) with arms extended
Level 1: 3 steps back, Rx movement
Level 2: 2 steps back, Rx movement
Level 3: 1 step back, Rx movement

**5.** **<u>3 x 4-6 Ring Flyes</u>** (protracted shoulders)

- Set top of the rings at knee height, take 3 steps forward
- Place wrists on top of the rings, not fingers
- Keep arms fully straight, lower down with control
- No scapular retraction, keep a more protracted position

Gloria, Sabrina, AnnaH, MarionW, Steven, DanielS, Robert, MichaelW (Marion's husband) Chris

Do the workout in the order as written.
Use a steady pace (don't go too fast at the beginning!)
Only scale if you absolutely have to!
If you can do at least 10 unbroken push-ups/sit-ups, do the workout Rx. Otherwise, scale these movements.
This is a long workout. Don't give up, take a break if you need one but finish the reps!
Even if you scale the moves, stay STRICT and make the last reps look EXACTLY like the first ones.

Push-Ups - Feet together, straight body & legs, hands under shoulders, elbows near to the body, chest touches the floor on EVERY rep!

Lunges- Step out far enough so that your knee stays above the ankle, NOT above the toes. (shins vertical) (nobody should scale these!)

Sit-Ups - Back straight, touch floor overhead and feet on return.

Airsquats - Hip below the knees on every rep. Stand straight up at the top so that the knees and hips are extended (nobody should scale these!)

coach_notes:
## Banded Back Squat Drill
Fix a Resistance Band (Black, Purple or Green) to the rig at just below knee height
Step inside the band and step back until you have some tension
Slowly lower into a squat, keep the back straight throughout
Drive the knees out against the band, especially at the bottom
The muscles you can feel working are your Hip Abductors, these should be activated on every rep of any squat

### Primary Hip Abductors

**Gluteus Medius**: A key stabilizer, preventing the opposite hip from dropping when you stand or walk on one leg. 

**Gluteus Minimus**: Sits beneath the medius, also supporting pelvic stability and abduction. 

**Tensor Fasciae Latae (TFL)**: Works with the gluteus maximus and attaches to the strong iliotibial band (ITB). 

### Secondary Hip Abductors

**Piriformis**: Helps with abduction and external rotation. 

**Sartorius**: The longest muscle in the body, also assists with hip flexion and rotation. 

**Gluteus Maximus (Superior Fibers)**: The largest gluteal muscle also contributes to abduction.

Skill/Strength- Turkish Get-Up (TGU) Full TGU video
3 things to master TGU (Wrist Position - top 2 knuckles are highest, Hand Position: 45°, don't move it!, Support Knee Position: 90° both legs

Breakdown (1 rep per side of each position with 2 secs hold to re-enforce the movement.)

This is normally 3 reps per side, but most people have already been through the basic instruction so should be able to perform the move without too much difficulty.)
Begin with light weight and use a heavier KB when technique is solid
KB TGU Base Floor Press - (2-count up and down) 
KB TGU Press To Roll - (4-count up and down, KB handle, wrist, elbow, shoulder & bottom shoulder in line)
KB 1/2 Turkish Get-Up to Elbow (6 count up and down, KB handle, wrist, elbow, shoulder & elbow in line, drive shoulders away from ears, push lats down)
KB Half Get-Up to Hand (8 count up and down, same as above with hand in line, drive shoulders away from ears, push lats down
KB Hip-Raise & Support Knee Placement (90° in knees, place back knee behind hand, torso upright, straight line between KB and hand on floor)
KB OH Kneel To Stand drive upwards, do not lean forwards from the hips, lead with the KB. 
When coming down trace a line down the bottom leg with the hand and place hand at 45° (roughly in line with or slightly behind the hand on the floor

coach_notes:
# Sumo DL Checklist:

Feet as wide apart as possible which still allows the shins to remain vertical, heels in line with the knees.

Point the feet towards the front end of the plates (about 30-40°)

Push the hips back (not down) as in a stiff-legged DL to lower down to the bar

When you reach the end range of motion, lower down by pushing knees towards the toes and lower the hips and grip the bar

Grip the bar so that the hands are under the shoulders or slightly outside, arms should be vertical (https://drive.google.com/file/d/18cyxfgCrkZS3fzZkNtlMat9PF1IZfVb_/view?usp=sharing)

If using alternate grip, make sure to change every set

Move the back into a neutral position and tighten the abs and lower back

Lean bodyweight slightly forward and make sure that the scapula, bar & mid-foot should be in a perfectly straight line
(https://drive.google.com/file/d/1znAkya57WBXVAcZfYAsHVnjHomVyrM6d/view?usp=sharing)

Pull the slack out of the bar and lift the 1st mm before performing the complete lift (no “Grip & Rip”) in an explosive manner, think “chest up”.

Drive the feet into the floor and the hips through at the top but don’t over extend

Concentrate on 3 isometric contractions:
Lumbar spine
Shoulder blades
Shoulders to pull bar close to body
(https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XsrD5y8EIKU&t=454s)

Drag the bar up the body in a perfectly straight line keeping it as close as possible
To finish, lock out the knees (straighten legs), lock out the hips (squeeze the glutes), lock out the shoulders by lifting the chest up
For 1rm Bar can be dropped after achieving lock-out, but beginners or people with poor form should also train the eccentric (negative) part of the movement

PoP:
Perform the whole movement in a slow, controlled manner, any shaky or jerky movements must be corrected before increasing weight
Hold the KB in the opposite hand to the standing leg

Keep the KB close to the body, don't reach forward
Touch the KB to the ground maintaining balance and control throughout

coach_notes:
Bench Press PoP:
Eyes under the bar
Set bar height so you don't have to reach up and lose shoulder position to move the bar out of the rack
Squeeze shoulder blades together
Arch the back and push the shoulders into the bench
Feet slightly out and back, full foot on the floor
Elbows set up under the bar or slightly forward but must come under the bar during the lift
Lower arms are roughly perpendicular to the bar
Take a deep breath in and un-rack the bar maintaining an arch in the back by pushing hard against the bench with the shoulders and pushing the feet into the floor
Grip the bar as tightly as possible and either try to rip it apart or bend it (depending which feels more powerful)
Lower the bar to the sternum taking about 2/3 seconds and push as explosively as possible on every rep
On the last rep, push the bar straight upwards first before pushing it backwards towards the rack
NOTE: Demo the job of the "spotter"

SETUP (teaching)
Hip to shoulder-width stance

Shoulders over or slightly in front of the bar
Bar in contact with the shins
Arms straight
Eyes on the horizon
EXECUTION (teaching)
Lumbar curve maintained
Hips and shoulders rise at the same rate until the bar passes the knees
Hips then extend
Bar moves over the middle of the foot
Heels down
Complete at full hip and knee extension
PoP (seeing)
Lumbar curve maintained
Weight on heels
Bar stays close to the frontal plane and body
Torso angle is relatively constant during the initial pull
Demonstrate active shoulders

Mens 94kg 2018 European Weightlifting Championship
https://youtu.be/ySnqYSmAJ9o

### Purpose/Focus
The muscle snatch is helpful to learn, reinforce and strengthen the proper upper body mechanics of the turnover (third pull) of the snatch. 
The power position variation helps focus on vertical leg drive and better bar-body interaction to improve bar proximity. The abbreviated motion can also be used to reduce the contribution of the legs and place more emphasis on upper body strength. 
From power position relative to dip will allow more focus on proper position, balance and leg drive.


### Muscle Snatch from Power Position - Execution:
Start standing tall with the bar hanging at arms’ length in your snatch grip. 
Ensure even whole foot balance and brace your trunk. 
Bend at the knees only with a vertical trunk just as you would for a jerk, actively pushing the bar against your hips, and stop.
After a 1-2 second pause, drive up with the legs and complete a muscle snatch.
Focus on keeping the bar as close as possible, finishing with strong vertical leg drive, and punching vertically into the overhead position at the end of the turnover.
It can be helpful to think of the movement as a snatch high-pull with an added turnover of the bar afterward. This will reinforce the idea of driving vertically and lifting the elbows high and out before the turnover.
The only difference between a dip muscle snatch and a muscle snatch from power position is that there is a pause in the bottom of the dip in the muscle snatch from power position.

* Snatch Drill: Slow Motion Snatch

Execution:
The slow-motion snatch is a simple technique exercise to help the lifter better adjust and feel positions and balance throughout the snatch
Begin with the feet in the receiving stance because they will remain planted on the floor. Perform a snatch at a very slow speed, maintaining continuous tension against the bar.
A typical error is to flip the elbows suddenly under the bar in the turnover—continue pulling against the bar and control the entire turnover.
If needed, the turnover itself can be performed at or closer to full speed.
Purpose:
The slow motion snatch is a basic technique exercise for the snatch to help the lifter better adjust and feel positions and balance throughout the lift

* Snatch Drill: Slow Motion Snatch

Execution:
The slow-motion snatch is a simple technique exercise to help the lifter better adjust and feel positions and balance throughout the snatch
Begin with the feet in the receiving stance because they will remain planted on the floor. Perform a snatch at a very slow speed, maintaining continuous tension against the bar.
A typical error is to flip the elbows suddenly under the bar in the turnover—continue pulling against the bar and control the entire turnover.
If needed, the turnover itself can be performed at or closer to full speed.
Purpose:
The slow motion snatch is a basic technique exercise for the snatch to help the lifter better adjust and feel positions and balance throughout the lift
Reps:
take the full 6 seconds to perform the Snatch being in motion throughout
3 x 6 secs PVC Pipe Slow Motion Snatch on coach's cue, review each athlete's position
3 x 3 (6 secs per rep) Slow Motion Snatch with empty bar
Record 3 rep max
Record 1 rep Max

Too many times athletes lift the weight above the head, then relax the core and drop the bar, this is how injuries can occur.

coach_notes:
**Supine Rope Climb - Scaled**
Lie under the rope with feet directly under the rope. Start with the knees bent and the feet flat on the floor about shoulder-width apart.
Climb up the rope, hand over hand until upright then return to the start position, hand under hand.
Keep the torso straight (between the shoulders and the hips should be a straight line)
The feet should not move from their starting position
**Supine Rope Climb**
Same as above but start with the body supine, legs straight out and only the heels on the floor.
Start to pull and keep body as straight as possible throughout the movement
Feet together is more difficult than feet apart
Place feet flat on the floor only at the top of the pull

Level 1
Sit with your back to the wall, butt about 1 hand-length away.
Place hands flat on the ground level with the butt.
Feet flat on the ground (tripod foot) knees at approx. 90°
Push ground away without raising the hips

Level 2
Place hands on Weight Plates or DBs, butt slightly further forwards, dig the heels into the ground and pull the feet backwards and down as you push the ground away
Make sure to relax the neck muscles

Level 3
Same but with straight legs

Level 4
Same but with raised feet on WP or platform
Raise slips slightly higher

Level 5
Same but hands and feet on the ground, raise hips higher without engaging the lower back

Level 6
Sam but with straight legs and body

Sprawl Frog Kick

Supine Rope Climb - Scaled
Lie under the rope with feet directly under the rope. Start with the knees bent and the feet flat on the floor about shoulder-width apart.
Climb up the rope, hand over hand until upright then return to the start position, hand under hand.
Keep the torso straight (between the shoulders and the hips should be a straight line)
The feet should not move from their starting position

Supine Rope Climb
Same as above but start with the body supine, legs straight out and only the heels on the floor.
Start to pull and keep body as straight as possible throughout the movement
Feet together is more difficult than feet apart
Place feet flat on the floor only at the top of the pull

1 x Rope Climb (Rx: Climb to touch tape mark, Sc = Supine Rope Climb)

6 x Sprawl Frog Kick (Sc: 3 reps with single knee-up and step back)
6 x Push-up Hand release (Sc: 3 reps)

coach_notes:
Skill/Strength Barbell Clean Points of Performance
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y42jmYe-g_E 
**Start position:**
Balance is on the whole foot
Back Extended (chest up & forward)
Brace ribcage
Knees out
Shoulders above or forwards of the bar
head & eyes forwards
**1st Pull:**
Push legs through whole foot
Maintain back angle
Keep bar close to legs
**2nd Pull**
Push legs vertically
Explode hips open (squeeze butt)
Guide bar back to hips
Finish shoulders behind hips
Guide bar close to body
**3rd Pull**
Pull elbows up & out briefly
Move feet to squat stance
Pull bar up and back towards shoulders
spin elbows around the bar
Reach chest up to meet the bar in the rack
Receipt of barbell
Balance on whole foot
Sit straight down into squat
Brace trunk even harder
Lift chest, shoulders & elbows up
**Recovery**
Brace trunk aggressively
Catch bounce in bottom of squat
Drive chest, shoulders & elbows up
Accelerate up maximally

* Snatch Drill: Slow Motion Snatch

Execution:
The slow-motion snatch is a simple technique exercise to help the lifter better adjust and feel positions and balance throughout the snatch
Begin with the feet in the receiving stance because they will remain planted on the floor. Perform a snatch at a very slow speed, maintaining continuous tension against the bar.
A typical error is to flip the elbows suddenly under the bar in the turnover—continue pulling against the bar and control the entire turnover.
If needed, the turnover itself can be performed at or closer to full speed.
Purpose:
The slow motion snatch is a basic technique exercise for the snatch to help the lifter better adjust and feel positions and balance throughout the lift
Reps:
take the full 6 seconds to perform the Snatch being in motion throughout
3 x 6 secs PVC Pipe Slow Motion Snatch on coach's cue, review each athlete's position
3 x 3 (6 secs per rep) Slow Motion Snatch with empty bar
Record 3 rep max
Record 1 rep Max

WOD CrossFit Benchmark Workout - The Girls "Isabel" -  CFH version - "Silent Isabel" 10 min Time Cap
30 Snatches  (♀ 43kg ♂ 61kg)

NOTES:
Load the bar with a weight with which you could perform 10 unbroken reps when fresh.
Aim to complete the workout between 3 and 6 minutes.
If you scale and complete it in under 3 minutes, the bar was too light.
 
The bar must not be dropped throughout the whole workout, hence "Silent". 
This enables the focus to remain on both the positive and negative part of the movement.
Too many times athletes lift the weight above the head, then relax the core and drop the bar, this is how injuries can occur.
